intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer clean, uncluttered interfaces that feel energetic rather than cozy or dark
facade.label: Bright & minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Explore lighter color palettes, generous whitespace, high contrast text. Users want clarity and simplicity, not atmospheric design.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer darker modes, more visual density, or warmer tones. Minimalism alone may feel cold or sterile to them.

facade.hypothesis: Users feel less cooking-anxiety when ingredient lists animate gradually rather than appear all at once
facade.label: Ingredients fade in
facade.acceptImplies: User values progressive disclosure and reduced cognitive load; may prefer gentle pacing throughout app
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immediate, complete information visibility; likely wants snappy, direct interactions over animated delays

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer clean, uncluttered interfaces that feel energetic rather than cozy or dark
evidence.implication: Explore lighter color palettes, generous whitespace, high contrast text. Users want clarity and simplicity, not atmospheric design.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer interfaces that feel casual and forgiving rather than clinical and precise
facade.label: Handwritten messiness
facade.acceptImplies: The app should embrace imperfection: sketchy fonts, loose layouts, hand-drawn icons, playful typos. It feels like a friend's notebook, not a corporate product.
facade.rejectImplies: The app should be clean, polished, and precise. Users want clarity and control, not charm masquerading as usability.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a friendly, playful voice that makes the process feel fun and low-stakes rather than intimidating
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality: friendly/playful vs. professional/serious
facade.label: Cheerful and casual
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into humor, conversational tone, encouraging language, emojis or playful visual language; the app should feel like a supportive friend, not a chef
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward more neutral, straightforward, or authority-driven voice; focus on efficiency and clarity over warmth; minimize personality in favor of pure functionality

facade.hypothesis: For people who hate cooking, a single metric view (cook time, servings, calories) beats a narrative recipe card
facade.label: Just the numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User wants minimal friction—show only what's essential to decide if a recipe fits their constraints. Favor stripped-down metric displays, ingredient counts, time estimates over descriptive prose.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants context and reassurance in the UI. They need story, tips, or human voice to feel confident. Favor conversational copy, guidance, anecdotes over bare data.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer large, scannable recipe cards over dense ingredient lists, reducing cognitive friction at a glance
facade.axisTargeted: Information density and visual hierarchy — sparse (card-based) vs dense (list-based)
facade.label: Giant recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore card-based layouts with big typography, whitespace, one recipe per view, minimal text density
facade.rejectImplies: Explore compact list views, tabs for ingredient/steps, high information density per screen, scrollable feeds

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum filtering control (dietary, time, skill filters)
Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum filtering control (dietary, time, skill filters)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer soft, approachable interface elements (rounded buttons, pill shapes) over sharp angular designs, because cooking feels intimidating and curved forms reduce perceived friction
facade.label: Rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore soft, friendly UI: rounded cards, pill buttons, curved dividers. Users want design that feels inviting, not sterile or technical.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer crisp, modern aesthetics with sharp edges and tight grids—design that feels efficient and minimal rather than warm.

draft.title: No-Fuss Recipes
draft.summary: A recipe app designed for people who want delicious meals with minimal cooking complexity. Focuses on quick, simple ingredients and straightforward instructions.
draft.html:
No-Fuss Recipes

Simple meals, minimal effort

Sheet Pan Chicken

20 min 3 ingredients

Throw chicken and vegetables on a pan. Roast. Done.

No-Cook Pasta Salad

10 min 5 ingredients

Mix pasta, tomatoes, and dressing. Chill. Serve cold.

Slow Cooker Tacos

6 hours 4 ingredients

Add meat and seasoning. Let it cook while you do other things.

draft.nextHint: Next decision point: Does the user want photo-heavy visual inspiration or text-focused simplicity? Show a recipe detail view with either rich food photography or minimal ingredient-first layout.
